meta.scope: full_repo (lint --all itself is scoped to shared/; audit walks repo-wide)
meta.invocation_note: lint --all completed exit=0 with no violations in shared/ (the scope the lint uses). Full-repo audit performed via direct pattern search using ripgrep + glob matching the exact lint rule regexes. MD-scope H-HEXA-CANONICAL regex uses \s which is non-portable on BSD (macOS) grep — lint would under-count on Mac, over-count on Linux. Counts below use ripgrep semantics (PCRE-like, same as Linux/H100 host).

BLOCKERS.reason: SSOT canonical asset registry lists 27 .py files as canonical 'file' entries. This is the exact scenario H-HEXA-CANONICAL was introduced for (2026-04-16 CLM 1B r3f lesson). If this file is used by any live runtime/SSOT loader, it actively violates hexa-first. BLOCKER because CLM/ALM runtime asset discovery may pull .py paths.

Non-empty hooks: PreToolUse runs .claude/hooks/block-forbidden-ext.sh, UserPromptSubmit runs inline /usr/bin/python3 -c. Violates H-NOHOOK (no hooks in settings.json) AND embeds bypass logic. BLOCKER because it subverts the entry.hexa autonomous dispatch model that the whole harness depends on.

DEBT.note: sim_bridge is research-heavy; many could add @hexa-first-exempt marker if truly system-wrapper, or port to .hexa. harness/evolve_gate.py is anomalous — harness should be .hexa-first.

Vast majority are comments inside .hexa files (TODO[pytorch], doc usage examples). Real executable bare-interp is concentrated in training/*.sh and ready/scripts/*.sh — those should be fixed first or wrapped with absolute /opt/conda/bin/python3.
Almost entirely training pipeline watchdogs (train_watchdog, monitor_and_test, h100_retrieve). Fix = -xf exact match or PID file. Non-urgent — patterns are unique enough that mis-kill risk is low.
shared/config/ has 5 prose .md files (CALCULATOR_RULES, GRADE_RUBRIC, hetzner_prompt, projects, loop/README). Convert to JSON/JSONL.
7 MD files reference python3 train_*.py. Warn-level; replace with $HEXA training/*.hexa.

surprising_finds.finding: ready/core/asset_registry.json contains 27 .py entries as canonical 'file' SSOT records
surprising_finds.impact: Exactly the scenario H-HEXA-CANONICAL was added to catch (2026-04-16). This file is the single biggest concentration of H-HEXA-CANONICAL error-severity violations in the entire repo.
surprising_finds.why_surprising: ready/ is supposedly the stage-for-ossification tree, but its core asset_registry references transitional .py paths. This is the canonical SSOT pointing to non-canonical assets.

surprising_finds.finding: ready/.claude/settings.json has active hooks (PreToolUse + UserPromptSubmit) despite H-NOHOOK policy
surprising_finds.impact: The ready/ tree has its own Claude settings that inject a bash hook subprocess running /usr/bin/python3 -c, violating both H-NOHOOK and (because the command is inline) R22-spirit.
surprising_finds.why_surprising: Main /Users/ghost/Dev/anima/.claude/settings.json has hooks={}, but the ready/ twin has full active hooks. Drift between stage-tree and main tree.

surprising_finds.finding: shared/harness/evolve_gate.py exists inside harness — the harness should be .hexa-first by its own rules
surprising_finds.impact: Harness breaking its own R1 policy, no @hexa-first-exempt marker.
surprising_finds.why_surprising: Harness authors generally enforce R1 strictly; this is an orphan. Most other harness .py files (option_ranker.py, growth_kpi_update.py) have the marker; evolve_gate.py does not.

priority_1_next_session.action: Re-point canonical 'file' fields to .hexa equivalents (training/train_alm.hexa, etc.). Keep .py as 'legacy_file' field if transitional. Est. 30 min for all 27 entries.
priority_1_next_session.why: Highest error-count concentration in a single SSOT file + this is the exact rule we just added today + CLM r3f learning. Fixing this one file resolves 27 violations and removes the biggest BLOCKER.

priority_2.action: Set hooks: {} and remove inline python3 limit-blocking hook. If limit-blocking is needed, route through harness/entry.hexa prompt_scan.
priority_2.why: Actively subverts harness autonomous-dispatch model across sub-tree.

priority_3.action: Convert to JSON/JSONL SSOT per AI-native policy.
priority_3.why: Small, bounded — quick win to bring R14 to 0.
